title: BlockPrintr
description: This mod converts scan files (.laz, .ply, and many more!) into block-based builds, printing 3D structures directly in your Minecraft world.
body:
<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube-nocookie.com/embed/m8cF4Uo2tRE" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" allowfullscreen></iframe>

#  

**BlockPrintr** converts 3D scan files into your Minecraft world in a few simple clicks. It supports various file formats, allowing you to bring the real world into the game.

There's other ways to put 3D objects into Minecraft, but our goal is to make it **SIMPLE** and **FREE**! We simplify the process by doing all the work for you, so without changing any settings you can already have a clean looking Minecraft representation.


Supported File Types:
- .glb
- .gltf
- .obj
- .laz
- .las
- .dxf
- .xyz
- .pts
- .ply


There's lots of customization features so that you can easily change how your structure looks in game. From different block palettes, to more advanced settings like shading and brightness adjustments.

The default hotkey to open the menu is "**ENTER**"

Simply drag a compatible scan file into the inputs folder, and then either click "Quick Place" or the "Edit" button to change block and color settings.
